name: Gothic
notes: Gothic (4th c. CE text; spoken in Iberia 5th–8th c.). East Germanic, earliest well-attested Germanic language. Wulfila's Bible (Codex Argenteus). VISIGOTHIC kingdom in Iberia ~470–711 CE; Gothic substrate loanwords in Ibero-Romance: guerra, guardar, ropa, yelmo. Gothic alphabet from Greek/Latin/runic. Conservative Germanic: no HG consonant shift. Labiovelar fricative /xʷ/ (ƕ). Voiced fricatives [β ð ɣ] as allophones of /b d g/.
